_notes: Keyed by CRS code (matches naptan_stations.crs). Lat/lon/tiploc already live in Postgres — don't duplicate here. Google Map link is NOT stored; construct at render time from naptan_stations lat/lon (see map.html) — Street View was removed from the station panel 2026-07-16 per the approved mockup. Any field can be null/empty until curated — sidebar should hide empty fields, not show placeholders. FIELD OWNERSHIP (see scripts/ for the population scripts): platforms/wheelchair/operators are written by scripts/fetch-osm-facts.mjs (OpenStreetMap, structured/physical — deterministic, no AI; wheelchair is still fetched/stored even though the panel stopped rendering an Accessibility field 2026-07-16). headline/opened_year/notable_features/managed_by/location are written by scripts/fetch-wikipedia-facts.mjs (Wikipedia, narrative/historical — Claude extracts, never invents; headline is a punchy 8-12 word sentence, not a paragraph — matches the locomotive spotlight's headline field). schema_jsonld is written by scripts/build-schema-jsonld.mjs (template assembly from the fields above, no new facts, no API calls — see that script's header). name/listed_status are manual/curated only — neither script writes them; wikipedia_title can also be auto-resolved by fetch-wikipedia-facts.mjs itself when missing (added 2026-07-13 — see that script's header for the confidence bar it requires before doing so), a human-set title is never overwritten. Existence/open-closed status is owned by the separate, pre-existing NaPTAN re-import pipeline. _osm/_wikipedia are provenance metadata (fetched_at, source id/title, license) written by their respective scripts — render wikipedia_url from wikipedia_title, don't store it; _wikipedia.sourceType="settlement-article" (set only for the NO_DEEP_LINK_KEYS case in fetch-wikipedia-facts.mjs) means the matched article is about the town/village the station sits in, not a dedicated station page — map.html renders that case as plain non-linked source text instead of a Wikipedia deep-link. photo (manual/curated only, 2026-07-16 station panel rework): {url, photographer, source, license, sourceUrl?} — photographer/source/license are REQUIRED together for the credit block to render at all (map.html's dbPhotoCreditHtml()), rendered as "{photographer} – {source} – {license}", e.g. "Richard Kelly from Manchester, England – Flickr – CC BY 2.0" — never a generic "Image via Wikipedia" line. sourceUrl (optional) makes the whole credit line link back to the original photo page. managed_by (added 2026-07-17) is the organisation managing the STATION ITSELF (e.g. "Network Rail"), distinct from operators (the TOCs whose services call there) — Wikipedia-infobox-tier-dependent, same coverage ceiling as opened_year, only ever populated for the substantive/geo-match-town-article tier. location (added 2026-07-17, same tier dependency) is the station's location as Wikipedia's infobox states it — usually a place/borough/county description, NOT a consistently-formatted postal address (confirmed live: only ~1 in 20 sampled articles gave a full street address+postcode; most give a general place name). Full postal-address coverage across all 2,637 stations regardless of Wikipedia tier would need a separate reverse-geocoding pipeline (e.g. Nominatim/OSM from NaPTAN's existing lat/lon) — parked as a future idea, not built. "Reference no." (a Historic England listed-building reference number, e.g. designation1_number in the infobox's heritage-designation block) was investigated 2026-07-16/17 and deliberately NOT added: the plain-text article extraction this script uses (explaintext=1) strips infobox templates entirely, so the actual reference number never reaches the text Claude sees — building this would need a new infobox-wikitext fetching pathway, and coverage would be sparse regardless (0/20 in a random sample of dedicated-content stations had any heritage-listing data at all — only stations whose building itself is listed have it). photo (2026-07-17: no longer manual/curated-only) is written by scripts/fetch-station-photos.mjs — deterministic, no AI (same discipline as fetch-osm-facts.mjs, not fetch-wikipedia-facts.mjs's Claude extraction): Wikipedia's REST summary lead image, then Wikimedia Commons' imageinfo API for real photographer/license/source attribution. Requires wikipedia_title already set (never guesses one), never overwrites an existing photo (human-set or from a prior run). {url, photographer, source, license, sourceUrl} — all four always present together when set; map.html's dbPhotoCreditHtml() requires photographer+source+license or renders nothing.
